era_context: Archaic Greece; Neo-Babylonian Empire; Spring and Autumn period in China. This year falls within the period of Solon's reforms or shortly after, depending on the precise dating. The broader historical context is one of institutional and legal innovation in the Mediterranean world.
geographic_coverage_gaps: Most of Asia outside China and the Near East
Most of the Mediterranean world outside Athens
disconfirming_evidence: No specific events are documented for 591 BCE in any surviving source. The year is historically obscure, and no disconfirming evidence can be identified because no claims about this year are made in the historical record.
historiographic_note: 591 BCE is essentially undocumented. No contemporary sources record events for this year. The historical record is silent. This year may have been historically significant, but if so, no evidence survives. Alternatively, it may have been a year of relative stability or insignificance in the regions for which we have documentation.
